item.content:encoded: Thank you so much everyone. Here's to many more :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mmh… Weird for me to say, but I didn't like this at all. The story seems formulaic, the main character is uninteresting. The way the story is told doesn't work for me, and the tone also seems off. The base skip if fine, if bland, and in my opinion it received some high praise that's not totally deserved, still worthy of an upvote. But this text misses the mark and just isn't good enough. Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was really emotional and even scary in a way. I also feel it nicely captured the feel of 2740 while also kind of adding to it. Personally I felt it was a bit long, but I enjoyed it nonetheless.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this should go on the main 2740 page

This is a short story, written as a traditional first person narrative, by a different author and based on one interpretation of an article that was deliberately ambiguous. In what way does that fit in the 2740 slot instead of what's there (which this is based on so it would also lose context besides)? How does that justify downvoting this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good writing, but this should go on the main 2740 page. -1, sadly.
Dunno when this hit +50, but I really am appreciative of it, given how great I think I did here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sure thing! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yup!

Thank you for your feedback, it's been very appreciated <33

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I Forget Myself In This Wondrous Night : D

Looking forward to reading it! :)

+ Show
- Hide

It's meant to show how the attic, or at least 2740, is persistent and continues to be persistent, something Olivia probably understands by that point.

OK, so it's like, even when she's recalling past events/dreams, she can still feel the attic calling out to her in the present. That is creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm curious to know what your favorite tale is. I'd check it out, if I haven't already read it.

I Forget Myself In This Wondrous Night : D

maybe that's what you like about it? I can see how that might give off a weirder/more ominous vibe.

Yeah.

+ show block
– hide block

It's meant to show how the attic, or at least 2740, is persistent and continues to be persistent, something Olivia probably understands by that point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad people are still finding my second-favorite tale

I'm curious to know what your favorite tale is. I'd check it out, if I haven't already read it.

That one is intentional, although I don't know if the meaning I meant to convey in that is as strong there or if it would be stronger if fixed/worded a different way.

I'm not sure if I get the meaning you're referring to.

I suggested the change because the sentence in question is in the present tense, while the ones before and after it are in the past tense, which makes it feel out of place - maybe that's what you like about it? I can see how that might give off a weirder/more ominous vibe.

Am I missing something else, though?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you! I adore this feedback a lot. I'm glad people are still finding my second-favorite tale : )

Fixed all of the grammar nitpicks except the last one. That one is intentional, although I don't know if the meaning I meant to convey in that is as strong there or if it would be stronger if fixed/worded a different way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn, this is really good.

Not only does this fit in seamlessly with the original article, it actually adds to it, without spoiling the mystery. And there were some fantastically creepy sentences in there, especially:

The ladder is only twelve rungs (I've been counting for a couple years now)

There's a force, but there's no force to really sense in there. It's empty in there. That's what I hope for.

I catch a glimpse of my parents, sleeping with their heads buried into the wall

I don't know what that last one means, but shit…

But my favorite has got to be this throwaway line here:

(did the door ever have a lock? I can't see why)

Such a subtle allusion to that sense you get in the original article that the parents did something to her.

My only criticism is that the first few paragraphs feel a bit info-dumpy to me - I get that the tale is being connected to the original article, but I think a few of the details that are reiterated from SCP- 2740 aren't necessary (like mentioning what her job is, for example).

+1

+ A few grammar nitpicks
- Hide nitpicks

Maybe if I at least think like that, part of the blame wouldn't be on me.

should be "won't be on me"; alternately, you could change "think" to "thought"

to clear the air up and make things better for the both of us.

suggest "for all of us" since you're referring to three people.

I snagged a few gifts for my parents and siblings on the way there, and it took me almost half a day of driving to get there.

suggest removing the first "there", since it's unnecessary and you'd avoid repeating it.

Everyone had acted like my existence was never there to begin with.

suggest "like I'd never existed to begin with"

The attic is still there, too.

should be "was still there"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fantastic. Hits me right in the creeps. The cognitive dissonance of the dream is a magnifying mirror on SCP-2740. Formal framework: gone. Creep factor unhindered!!

+1. <ok, I gush now>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would chastise you for craving upvotes, but my frail ego demands them even more hungrily than you do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: (psst you didnt +1 j/s)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn, Deci, you write good shit. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good job on capturing the same tone and feel as the SCP it's about. This lowers the spinal temperature from chilling to frigid. It's also neat to see it something more in depth from her perspective. This fits very well.

item.title: wow
item.content:encoded: This is one of the good ones detail,depth,and a big story to tell.Also…it's worth mentioning that this is about SCP-2740.

item.title: Author's Comment
item.content:encoded: New tale based on SCP-2740! Thank you to kaktus for his approval on writing the tale, and to everyone in chat and on the forums for looking at it, as well as Dr Leonard and Roget for providing feedback (sorry for not incorporating more of your feedback Roget; I just couldn't figure out how to :c ).
